Provided comfort with lines and tubes
Patient will be able to maintain clear and patent airway
Patient will breathe comfortably without acute dyspnea
Patient will have adequate gas exchange on FiO2 < 60% and PEEP < 10
Patient will not have aspiration event
Assess RR pattern, oxygenation, and auscultation
Culture sputum and blood for new fever
Oral care every 2-4 hours, remove all debris
PEG or NGT placement for aspiration risk
Infection will be recognized early to allow for prompt treatment
Patient will remain free from infection as measured by labs, VS, absence of purulent drainage
Administer and / or teach use of antimicrobial (antibiotic) drugs as ordered
Assess for redness, swelling, increased pain or purulent drainage at any site
Dispose of contaminated material per hospital protocol
Emphasize proper use of PPE by all staff and visitors
Follow hospital policy for appropriate precautions as indicated
Maintain aseptic technique for any procedures
Monitor and assess urine and stool appearance
Monitor and report VS, labs, etc. of concern
Practice proper hand hygiene and teach others
Identify delirium as soon as clinically evident
Patient will remain alert and oriented per patient's pre-hospital baseline
Patient will remain free of signs and symptoms of agitation
Patient will remain safe and free of injury
Risk factors will be minimized or eliminated
Assess CAM-ICU every 12 hours and PRN
